channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇳 Tata-ASML Partnership to Build India's First Semiconductor Fab
item.description: Tata Electronics and Dutch chip equipment maker ASML signed an agreement to build India's first front-end semiconductor fabrication plant in Gujarat, marking a major milestone in the country's push for chip manufacturing independence.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Launches Largest Drone Attack on Moscow in Over a Year
item.description: Ukraine fired around 600 drones at Russia overnight in its biggest attack on Moscow in more than a year, killing at least four people. Russia's defense ministry said air defenses intercepted drones across 14 regions including the capital.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's "Vulture's Advantage" Explored Amid Middle East War
item.description: The Atlantic analyzes Iran's strategic calculus in the ongoing conflict, noting that Tehran's high tolerance for suffering gives it asymmetric leverage against wealthier Gulf neighbors whose economies are vulnerable to disruption.

item.title: ⚔️ Live Updates: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Iran Says It Cannot Trust Americans
item.description: CBS News provides ongoing coverage of the Middle East conflict as Israel continues strikes on Lebanon and Iran declares it cannot trust the US, while FIFA officials hold constructive talks with Iranian federation about World Cup participation.

item.title: 🇦🇪 Fire breaks out near Abu Dhabi nuclear plant after drone strike
item.description: A fire erupted near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in Abu Dhabi following a drone strike, though Emirati officials confirmed no radiation risks. The incident comes amid heightened regional tensions with Iran.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇱 US Army leaders questioned over Poland deployment cancellation
item.description: Lawmakers grilled Army officials over the timing and reasons behind a cancelled Poland deployment, with Rep. Don Bacon calling it a "terrible signal" to NATO allies at a critical moment for European security.

item.title: 🤖🇱🇻 NATO tests ground combat robots near Russian border in Latvia
item.description: NATO forces exercised in Latvia's dense forests this week, testing ground robots in combat scenarios near the Russian border as the alliance explores autonomous warfare capabilities.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Macron Opens Africa Summit in Kenya With $27 Billion Investment Package
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced €23 billion in investments at the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, marking France's first major summit in an Anglophone African country. The move signals a shift from colonial-era dominance toward "partnership of equals" as France seeks new allies.

item.title: 🛢️ Coal Makes Global Comeback as Iran War Cuts Off LNG Supplies
item.description: Countries worldwide are returning to coal power after the Iran war effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, cutting off about 20% of global liquefied natural gas supplies. The shift is most pronounced in Asia as nations prioritize energy security over climate goals.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Italy PM Meloni visits Modena after car-ramming attack injures several
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni cancelled a planned meeting with Cyprus president to travel to Modena after a car plowed into pedestrians in an incident authorities are investigating. Four bystanders tackled the knife-wielding driver after the attack.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran stock market to reopen Tuesday after conflict suspension
item.description: Iran will reopen its stock market on Tuesday following a suspension during the conflict with the US and Israel, IRNA news agency reported. The market closure was one of the economic impacts of the Gulf War.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Palestinian President Abbas son elected to top Fatah leadership body
item.description: Yasser Abbas, the 64-year-old son of aging Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas, won a seat on Fatahs central committee in elections seen as a potential succession move. The vote comes amid broader Palestinian political uncertainty.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland blindsided by email glitch on US troop movements
item.description: Poland's military chief received alerts about US troop relocations via email, revealing coordination failures as American forces shift. The incident raises questions about NATO communication protocols.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Tens of thousands protest in London across two major demonstrations
item.description: Massive crowds marched through central London on Saturday in separate protests over immigration policy and economic conditions, highlighting growing social tensions in Britain.

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore deepfake scam steals $3.8 million via fake PM video call
item.description: A sophisticated scam using deepfake technology to impersonate Singapore Prime Minister Wong and President Tharman in a Zoom call conned a victim out of millions. Police have obtained footage of the conference.

item.title: 🇺🇸 New York's Long Island Rail Road shut down by union strike
item.description: North America's largest commuter rail system halted operations Saturday as unions launched a strike after stalled contract negotiations, disrupting travel for thousands of commuters.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine launches massive drone attack on Moscow region
item.description: Russia says it intercepted over 500 Ukrainian drones in one of the largest attacks of the war, killing at least three in the Moscow region as Ukraine escalates strikes deep inside Russian territory.

item.title: 🌍🚨 WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern after over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from a rare Bundibugyo strain with no approved vaccine.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea seeks emergency arbitration to block Samsung strike
item.description: South Korea will pursue all options including emergency arbitration to prevent a labor strike at Samsung Electronics, the country's largest employer, as the government prioritizes economic stability.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties with Nordic Nations
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is strengthening alliances between "middle powers" as the United States becomes a less reliable partner, part of broader efforts to counter Russian and Chinese activity in the Arctic.

item.title: 🇸🇱 Sierra Leone to Accept Hundreds of Deported West Africans from US
item.description: The deal is the latest in a series of agreements by the Trump administration to accelerate removals of undocumented immigrants from the United States.

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvia Nominates New Prime Minister After Government Resignation
item.description: President Edgars Rinkevics proposed opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister following Evika Silina's resignation.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Tata and ASML Sign Major Semiconductor Deal During Modi Netherlands Visit
item.description: India's Tata Group and Dutch chip equipment giant ASML signed a semiconductor cooperation agreement as Prime Minister Modi visited the Netherlands, with talks also focusing on defense and security cooperation.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Young Kyiv Couple Killed in Russian Airstrike Had Hoped to Start Family
item.description: Mourners in Kyiv remembered a young couple killed in a Russian airstrike, one of many civilian casualties as Russia continues its bombardment of Ukrainian cities.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Threatens to Block All Military Equipment Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran declared it will no longer allow "enemy" military equipment through the strategic Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate with Israel and the US, potentially disrupting global oil shipments.
